specific_location: High Plains Sportsmans Club pond off Highway 71 just south of the Lincoln County line.
description: I viewed a large black bird about the size of a smaller heron or egret. The bird was almost totally black except around the bill.  The bird had a long tail that was flaired, fanned or rounded; delicate, thin but longish bill that contrasted sharply to nearby Black-crowned Night Herons; and thin neck.  The head was rounded and lacked any crests or white plumes. The face was marked by a small yellow patch below the bill that did not extend markedly down on the throat (however, no white border was observed). Above the bill the bird showed dark lores.Behaviors: The bird was perched in a dead tree on the side of a small island in a large pond. It appeared to be sleeping or resting with its head hidden at first, but when a handful of Black-crowned Night-herons flew up into the dead tree it became agitated and threatening; perhaps hissing at the night-herons. The bird in question was about the same length as the night herons.Call: nonePlumage: adult basic
